item.content:encoded: no little buddy. the place change has not beated you. you are very good at the helping words. please make a tale with more crabchats. (quiet sobbing)

item.content:encoded: I will, I really will do it

Come on people, gotta step it up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, I'm so glad that you liked it! I'm a big fan of wholesome SCPs, I'm glad it made someone else smile. ^^

item.title: Re: 🦀🦀Crabnet is Here!🦀🦀
item.content:encoded: c r a b

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I usually read these before going to bed. A majority of the time they're just okay. Sometimes I get "jesus christ why the hell did I read that I'm never sleeping again" bedtime stories. This was a happy bedtime story with a happy ending. So thanks for that! +1

item.title: 🦀🦀Crabnet is Here!🦀🦀
item.content:encoded: Crab

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lol Crabnet goi would be the fucking best

I’ve had people tell me to do the Lobster Forums and start The Crab War haha

I’m glad you like it! Crabs best

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Five years from now I'm going to finish up a GOI-Format for Crabnet, add it to the Crabnet Hub, navigate to the webpage of the Scuttle, Crab, Program Foundation, revisit this article and say wistfully to myself, "Ah, so this is where it all started."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I love that people like my wholesome crabs ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so wholesome. A definite nice refresher to the other serious new SCPs. I love it <333

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're like the 17th person to bring up the crab rave when this SCP was mentioned lol

c r a b r a v e

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you! I actually was looking into writing a tale about this SCP simply due to how much I loved it.

And awe ;;;;;; I am so flattered it inspired you to think about more of what would happen, and yeah that would be interesting lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When this SCP breaches containment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so charming, and feels like an older series of SCP, before the recent trend of "EVERYTHING-IS-ON-FIRE" style entries.

I wish there was a whole page of the crab communique to really flesh out thier feelings and personalities. This article demands an extended experiment/interview log.

I already have a bunch of head cannon swirling around about "practical" use for this skip in the event of containment breach/lockdown, and how -1 would add a lot of intresting flavor in such an event.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded: The crabs not understanding big words wasn't supposed to be filler, I just felt it was realistic. Anyway, thanks for your feed back. I'll keep it in mind for future projects.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't an especially big fan of this. The typing style of the crabs was, despite understanding the effect you were going for, annoying at best to read through. When combined with the sheer randomness of the first message section, the irritation massively outweighed what cuteness was apparent.

In the second section, the sort of bitter-sweetness and wholesomeness didn't really complement each other especially well. The ethics/effects of containment are hardly a new topic in the Foundationverse, and with the crabs' acceptance of this new home just because of the power of friendship, it definitely verged on being overly saccharine. Anything which could properly evoke any emotions either way wasn't developed enough, and was also drowned out by the filler content of the crabs not understanding any complex words, which was neither entertaining nor beneficial to my experience.

Overall, I feel this leant too hard on the cuteness/wholesomeness aspect of childlike beings communicating to each other, and focused more on the irritating or mildly amusing at best sections while neglecting real emotional impacts or intriguing narrative progression.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CRABNET

I AM NOT CUTE CATBIRD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, I like this one. It's intriguing and also kind of funny. I love the idea of a bunch of cute animals having social media.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the catches, I'll make those changes! and thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like that the ending of this manages to be both charming and wistful. I'm a little disappointed that we didn't get to see 5090-C in action :<

I ought to point out some of the linguistic errors which I felt detracted from the description. Hope you don't mind!

+ Open
- Close

A faraday cage

"Faraday" should be capitalised

No evidence of external hardware which operates SCP-5090 exists, but is instead entirely operated by SCP-5090-1.

This makes it sound like the verb "operated" refers to the nonexistent external hardware. A possible rewording would be: "No evidence of external hardware which operates SCP-5090 exists; instead, SCP-5090 is entirely operated by SCP-5090-1."

One or more of these websites will appear in a users browser history and or bookmarks

user's (with apostrophe), and/or (with slash)

SCP-5090-A is a blog style

blog-style (hyphenated)

Despite it's intended purpose, SCP-5090-B contains

its (no apostrophe)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Old Response
Close

I don't really know a lot about IT ;; that was suggested to me, in all honesty. Most of the IT was. I'm not sure what to change it to, or if I should take it out, cause I want this to be accurate.

Edit: I removed:

SCP-5090-1 will latterly fold their eyestalks repeatedly. Metananalysis of the pattern has led to the conclusion that SCP-5090 is running on a server similar to that of an Oracle Server.

entirely, as it doesnt seem needed in the long run.

Thank you so much! Thanks again for the advice:)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Old feedback
Close

Did you have someone check the IT here?

Metananalysis of the pattern has led to the conclusion that SCP-5090 is running on a server similar to that of an Oracle Server.

As politely as possible, this made me gag a little bit. Oracle is a database infrastructure that you're going to run on top of something else like a Linux or Unix server, it is not "a server" in and of itself. This was enough of a smack to the face that I did not continue reading the piece.

I'd also add…

SCP-5090-1 will latterly fold their eyestalks repeatedly.

This is really janky just hanging on its own there.

I'm interested in reading the rest of this piece but we have to start off on good footing if I am going to be able to get there. No vote right now.

This reads much better without that earlier stuff getting in the way. Part charming, part whistful. Some of the approach of the logs were initially tough for me to follow but after getting used to it I didnt mind and the format did not stop me from enjoying. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
crabnet.

crabnet.

fastest net.

fierce.

item.content:encoded: This is just… wholesome. Not very mysterious, scary or unnerving. Just heartwarming. I love it just like this, though!

item.content:encoded: This is adorable. +1 for being adorable.

item.title: crab
item.content:encoded: doin me a crus-ta-tion

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Welp, I'm not sure if this is gonna do too hot, but I love it

Thank you to HammerMaiden for the idea, and challenging me to pursue it! Also thanks to MalyceGraves, HammerMaiden, and a few others for crit!
